title: arthropteris boutoniana
intro: Arthropteris boutoniana, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Nephrolepidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems due to its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content. The health benefits of Arthropteris boutoniana include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from various health conditions.
